senses.definition: a short, witty, and concise joke or humorous observation that is delivered in a single line

examples.x: The comedian delivered a brilliant one-liner that had the audience in stitches.
She's known for her clever one-liners that always leave people laughing.
His repertoire includes a variety of one-liners that he uses to warm up the crowd before his set.
The comedy show featured a competition for the best one-liner, with contestants vying for the title.
The comedy special was filled with memorable one-liners that became instant classics among fans.

senses.definition: (Yiddish) a comedian's distinctive style, routine, or comedic gimmick that sets them apart from others

examples.x: The comedian's shtick involves self-deprecating humor and witty observations about everyday life.
Her shtick revolves around impersonating famous celebrities and poking fun at their quirks and mannerisms.
He's known for his absurdist shtick, often weaving surreal and nonsensical elements into his comedy routines.
The comedian's shtick of using props and visual gags adds an extra layer of humor to his performances.
While some comedians rely on shock value, her shtick is more about clever wordplay and clever observations.

senses.definition: a venue where comedians perform stand-up comedy routines to a live audience

examples.x: They went to a comedy club to watch a stand-up comedian perform.
The comedy club was packed with people laughing at every joke.
She has a gig at a local comedy club this weekend.
He dreams of performing at a famous comedy club in New York.
They enjoy visiting the comedy club whenever they need a good laugh.

senses.definition: a standard address by a politician during a campaign, outlining key policies and reasons for running

examples.x: The candidate delivered their stump speech, focusing on education reform and healthcare access.
During the campaign, the politician emphasized economic revitalization and job creation in their stump speech.
The candidate's stump speech addressed issues like climate change and social justice.
At each stop, the politician tailored their stump speech to local concerns.
The candidate's stump speech outlined a vision of unity, progress, and opportunity for all.

senses.definition: the host and organizer of a public event who introduces acts and ensures the smooth flow of activities
the practice of a performer engaging with and interacting with the audience during a live performance, often in a comedic or improvisational manner
the act of plagiarizing or stealing jokes or comedic material from another comedian without giving proper credit or permission
a comedian who relies on tired, overused, or clich√©d material, often resulting in unoriginal or predictable jokes
a well-rehearsed and polished set of five minutes of material that a comedian can perform reliably in front of an audience
comedy content that contains explicit, vulgar, or risqu√© language, themes, or topics that are considered more provocative, edgy, or offensive in nature
a short, humorous anecdote, joke, or punchline intended to elicit laughter

examples.x: The comedian delivered a series of hilarious gags about everyday life, drawing laughter from the crowd.
Her stand-up routine was filled with clever gags and witty observations that kept the audience entertained throughout.
He opened his set with a quick gag to break the ice and set the tone for the rest of the performance.
The comedy special featured a variety of gags, ranging from quick one-liners to extended comedic sketches.
During the comedy show, the comedian's playful delivery of gags had the audience roaring with laughter.

senses.definition: an individual in an audience who interrupts a performance, typically a comedian or speaker, by making loud or disruptive comments, criticisms, or jeers

examples.x: The comedian handled the heckler with grace, cleverly incorporating their remarks into the act and diffusing the situation with humor.
The speaker paused momentarily to address the heckler, politely asking them to refrain from interrupting the presentation.
Despite efforts to maintain order, the heckler persisted in making disruptive comments, prompting security to escort them out of the venue.
Some comedians thrive on heckler interactions, skillfully engaging with them to create spontaneous and memorable moments during their performances.
The heckler's outburst disrupted the flow of the event, drawing attention away from the main speaker and causing discomfort among the audience members.

senses.definition: a joke or reference understood only by a specific group, often based on shared experiences or knowledge

examples.x: The sitcom featured many in-jokes that only longtime fans of the series would understand, adding an extra layer of humor for dedicated viewers.
Among the group of friends, there were numerous in-jokes stemming from shared memories and experiences, bringing them closer together.
The comedian's set included several in-jokes about the local community, eliciting laughter from residents who recognized the references.
The in-joke between the siblings brought back fond memories of their childhood, evoking laughter and nostalgia.
While the movie contained many in-jokes aimed at fans of the original franchise, newcomers to the series found themselves puzzled by the references.

senses.definition: something a person says that is intended to make others laugh

examples.x: He told a funny joke that made everyone at the party laugh.
She couldn't stop giggling after hearing her friend's silly joke.
The comedian's joke about the airplane food had the audience in stitches.
They shared a private joke that only they understood.
His attempt at a joke fell flat, and no one found it amusing.

senses.definition: one's face when it is devoid of any signs of amusement, interest, or even sadness

examples.x: I'm trying to keep a straight face while telling him the silly story.
I couldn't keep a straight face when remembering how she fell in the mud.
I'm forcing a straight face while she recounts the silly mishap.
She'll struggle holding a straight face when I retell that ridiculous story.
She would manage a straight face if it were anything else, not that anecdote.
